description: Perform a Recency, Frequency, Monetary (RFM) analysis on the Sakila customer database to identify high-value customers at risk of churning. Create a table named customer_rfm_scores in the sakila database with columns customer_id, recency_days, frequency_count, monetary_total, r_score, f_score, and m_score. Populate this table for all 599 customers using the reference date '2006-02-14 23:59:59', calculating scores (1-5) using NTILE(5) with customer_id ASC as a tie-breaker. R_Score 5 represents the most recent activity (lowest days), F_Score 5 the highest frequency, and M_Score 5 the highest spend. Export a list of 'High Value Churn Risks' (defined as R_Score <= 2 AND (F_Score >= 4 OR M_Score >= 4)) including columns customer_id, email, r_score, f_score, and m_score to /home/ga/Documents/exports/churn_risk_customers.csv.
